title.en: GENERIC PROCESSES OR APPARATUS FOR THE MANUFACTURE OR TREATMENT OF DEVICES COVERED BY CLASS H10
Bonding of wafers, substrates or parts of devices
{Bonding of semiconductor wafers or semiconductor substrates to semiconductor wafers or semiconductor substrates (preparing SOI wafers using bonding H10P90/1914)}
{Bonding of semiconductor wafers to insulating substrates}
Formation of materials, e.g. in the shape of layers or pillars
of semiconductor materials
of conductive or resistive materials
of insulating materials
Ion implantation into wafers, substrates or parts of devices
into semiconductor materials, e.g. for doping
into insulating materials
Diffusion of dopants within, into or out of wafers, substrates or parts of devices (during formation of materials H10P14/00)
Diffusion of dopants within, into or out of semiconductor bodies or layers
Diffusion for doping of insulating layers
Diffusion for doping of conductive or resistive layers
Irradiation with electromagnetic or particle radiation of wafers, substrates or parts of devices
{with corpuscular radiation}
for inducing a nuclear reaction transmuting chemical elements
with high-energy radiation
Gettering within semiconductor bodies
{within silicon bodies}
Intrinsic gettering, i.e. thermally inducing defects by using oxygen present in the silicon body
Etching of wafers, substrates or parts of devices
Dry etching; Plasma etching; Reactive-ion etching
Wet etching
{using masks for semiconductor materials}
{using masks for conductive or resistive materials}
{using masks for insulating materials}
Grinding, lapping or polishing of wafers, substrates or parts of devices
Electromechanical polishing [EMP]; Electrochemical mechanical polishing [ECMP]
Chemomechanical polishing [CMP] (electrochemical mechanical polishing H10P52/20)
Cutting or separating of wafers, substrates or parts of devices
by laser cutting
by forming weakened zones for subsequent cutting or separating, e.g. by laser treatment or by ion implantation
by sawing, e.g. using revolving or reciprocating blades
by scoring, breaking or cleaving
Auxiliary processes or arrangements
Debonding of wafers, substrates or parts of devices
Singulating wafers or substrates into multiple chips, i.e. dicing
{comprising two or more processes, e.g. etching and cutting}
Cleaning of wafers, substrates or parts of devices
{Cleaning before device manufacture, i.e. Begin-Of-Line process}
{Cleaning during device manufacture}
{Cleaning after the substrates have been singulated}
{Cleaning for reclaiming}
{characterised by the part to be cleaned}
{Cleaning only by mechanical processes}
{Cleaning only by lasers processes, e.g. laser ablation}
{Cleaning only by supercritical fluids}
Handling or holding of wafers, substrates or devices during manufacture or treatment thereof
{Apparatus for manufacture or treatment}
{Apparatus for monitoring, sorting, marking, testing or measuring}
using carriers specially adapted therefor, e.g. front opening unified pods [FOUP]
for conveying, e.g. between different workstations
for positioning, orientation or alignment
for supporting or gripping
Testing or measuring during manufacture or treatment of wafers, substrates or devices
characterised by the properties tested or measured, e.g. structural or electrical properties
{characterised by multiple measurements, corrections, marking or sorting processes}
{Structural arrangements therefor}
Manufacture or treatment of masks on semiconductor bodies, e.g. by lithography or photolithography
of masks comprising organic materials
of masks comprising inorganic materials
Preparation of wafers not covered by a single main group of this subclass, e.g. wafer reinforcement
{Preparing bulk and homogeneous wafers}
{Preparing inhomogeneous wafers}
{by transferring two-dimensional materials}
{by transferring layers from a donor substrate to a final substrate utilising a temporary handle substrate as an intermediary}
{by concurrent transfer of multiple parts}
Generic processes or apparatus for manufacture or treatments not covered by the other groups of this subclass
{Planarisation of semiconductor materials}
{Planarisation of conductive or resistive materials}
{Planarisation of inorganic insulating materials}
{Planarisation of organic insulating materials}
{Separation of active layers from substrates}
Treatments of semiconductor bodies to modify their internal properties, e.g. to produce internal imperfections
{Alloying conductive materials with semiconductor bodies}
Mechanical treatments, e.g. by ultrasounds
Chemical treatments
Electrical treatments, e.g. for electroforming
Thermal treatments, e.g. annealing or sintering
{Formation of n- or p-type semiconductors, e.g. doping of graphene}
{Hydrogenation or deuterisation, e.g. using atomic hydrogen from a plasma}
